name: Swedish Nuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Set aside a stick of butter to melt in a flat pan.
2. Preheat the oven to 300°F (150°C).
3. Separate the eggs; sprinkle a pinch of salt onto the egg whites. Reserve the yolks for custards if desired.
4. Beat the egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add sugar, cinnamon, and vanilla extract while continuing to beat.
5. Gently fold in the pecan halves.
6. Pour the mixture over the melted butter (do not stir it in) and spread evenly.
7. Cook for 15 minutes. Remove the pan from heat and stir the nuts gently.
8. Return the pan to the heat and cook another 10 minutes, stirring again.
9. Repeat steps 7-8 until the nuts are separated and all the butter is absorbed (approximately one hour in total).
Be patient throughout the process. Initially, it may look unappealing, but it will work out well. While other nuts like almonds or walnuts could theoretically be used, I found them disappointing in this recipe.
